description:
MDI uses OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials with **no refresh tokens** — every refresh is a full credential exchange. Tokens are cached in Redis and shared across all worker processes.

**Scope**
- `src/lib/mdi/token.ts` — single source of truth for MDI auth tokens.
  - `getToken()`: read from Redis. If TTL <60s remaining or missing, fetch a new one via `POST /partner/auth/token`. SETEX with `TTL = expires_in - 60s`.
  - `forceRefresh()`: blow the cache and re-fetch. Called by the HTTP client on a 401 response.
  - Concurrency: use a Redis lock (`SET NX EX 30`) to serialize concurrent refreshes — N workers shouldn't slam MDI with N parallel token requests.
- `mdi-token-prewarm` scheduled job (registered by T-046) calls `getToken` every 30 min to keep the cache warm.
- Credentials sourced from Secrets Manager (`apoth/<env>/mdi/client_credentials`).
- Boot-time sentinel: secret's `env` field must match `APP_ENV` (per T-041).

**Acceptance**
- Concurrent worker tasks performing first-time token fetch result in exactly one `POST /partner/auth/token` (locked).
- A 401 from MDI triggers exactly one refresh + retry; second 401 fails loud (alarm).
title: MDI token client (Redis cache + refresh-on-401)
